title: [v4.0-rhel] CI maintenance mode
body:
This release was originally built around Fedora 35 and golang 1.16.
However the RHEL platform this branch feeds has since moved on to golang
1.17 out of necessity to support security backports.  Given the
difficulty/complexity of updating the CI platform to F36 (golang 1.18),
and the age of this branch, place it into a slimmed-down
"maintenance-only" mode.

Note: This implies a heavier reliance on downstream QE/QA.

Ref: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22269

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: I did not expect to see the Netavark tests here.  That's way later than v4.0.  I wonder if that's part of the gum up?

@Luap99 Tom's trying to do [a bunch of CVE backports](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22269) that require golang 1.17+.  However this release branch's CI was only designed around F35 and golang 1.16.  In this PR, I tried grabbed a newer F36 CI VM image (which uses 1.18) but the tests are very much not happy.

Since this is an old release branch, some of the tasks we can disable safely.  But I'm uncomfortable with bumping the images w/ so many networking-related integration test failures.  Can you see (or think of) anything that might be to blame?

Netavark was added in 4.0 so it very much makes sense to have them.
I have no idea what is going wrong here, I can properly spend more time to figure it out but is that really worth it? All I can guess is that make install or whatever is failing and does not add rootlessport/podman in the right place based on the errors. dnsname (podman-plugins) seems also to be missing.

We can accept that this branch is too old to test upstream and removing everything but basic build testing, relying on QE and gating tests to ensure that backports do not break things. Or we can spend a large amount of time trying to resurrect CI on the branch.

> but is that really worth it?

Probably not.

> spend a large amount of time trying to resurrect CI on the branch.

I'm in favor of Minimizing CI.  The golang 1.16 "barrier" is really really tall in my experience attempting something similar in buildah.

If there are no objections, I'll re-work this PR to minimize CI instead of trying to enhance it.
Works for me @cevich.  @ypu we will need to be stringent with testing Podman v4.0.* going forward.
LGTM but PR is still marked as draft so it cannot be merged, @cevich is this good to go in?
Oh it worked!  That's a surprise.  I'll un-draft it, but am slightly nervous this set of tasks is too big.  I'll happily trim this down further if anyone thinks so.

Let's see if this does the job...
/lgtm
